$comment: D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-MULTI-ARTIFACT (c171): the FIRST multi-artifact corpus example, across THREE targets. Each target's `dependsOn` builds `dsslib.c` (exporting dss_lib_answer) into a STATIC LIBRARY via the c171-D `-staticlib` format (a `.lib` on pe64 / a `.a` on elf64 x86_64 + aarch64), then builds `main.c` (extern dss_lib_answer) RESOLVING against it via --resolve-library -> the c169/c170 COFF / c165 ELF static-link pulls the member into a SELF-CONTAINED exec -> exit 42. Witnesses, at the corpus tier + end-to-end: the c171 dependsOn runner capability, the c171-D staticlib driver surface, and the static-link pull/merge. RUNTIME per leg: PE runs natively on the Windows gate; ELF x86_64 runs natively under WSL / on the ubuntu leg; ELF aarch64 runs under qemu-aarch64 (the emulator) / on the ubuntu-24.04-arm leg -- the arm64 arm is the runnable witness that CLOSES D-LK-STATIC-LINK-AARCH64-RUNTIME (arm64 static-link had been build-only). SkippedCrossHost where a leg cannot run, with the BUILD still giving structural coverage.
